description:
Rewrite `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit-napi/src/actor_factory.rs` to host a Rust-side receive loop that translates `rivetkit-core` `ActorEvent`s into TSF invocations against the existing callback shape used in `feat/sqlite-vfs-v2`. The NAPI layer becomes the emulation boundary for every callback core does not expose (`onCreate`, `createState`, `createVars`, `createConnState`, `onMigrate`, `onWake`, `onBeforeActorStart`, `onStateChange`, `onBeforeActionResponse`, `run`). Public TS actor-authoring API stays 1:1 with `feat/sqlite-vfs-v2`. Full spec at `.agent/specs/rivetkit-napi-receive-loop-adapter.md` (READ THIS FIRST every iteration), plus the core-side contract in `.agent/specs/rivetkit-core-receive-loop-api.md`.

===== SCOPE: READ BEFORE EVERY STORY =====

ALLOWED EDITS:
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit-napi/` (primary â€” adapter loop, NAPI surface)
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/actor/instance/state-manager.ts`
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/actor/instance/mod.ts` (only for ctx-wiring hooks)
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/actor/conn/state-manager.ts`
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/actor/conn/connection-manager.ts` (only for conn-hibernation plumbing)
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/registry/native.ts` (wire `buildNativeFactory` to new callbacks)
  - `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-core/` (ONLY for the three event-shape renames + additive ctx helpers + inspector attach/detach/debouncer/broadcast described in US-001..US-004)
  - `rivetkit-rust/engine/artifacts/errors/` (for generated RivetError JSON only)

FORBIDDEN:
  - `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit/` (Rust wrapper â€” has its own separate migration)
  - `rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-wasm/`, `workflow-engine`, other TS packages
  - `engine/`, `packages/`, `shared/`, `self-host/`, `scripts/`, `website/`, `examples/`, `frontend/`
  - `envoy-client`, other workspace crates

Do NOT introduce `ActorEvent` or `Reply<T>` into the JS surface. Do NOT change the TS public actor-authoring API. Do NOT change the wire protocol, KV layout, inspector HTTP API, or engine startup plumbing.

===== SPEED RULES =====

- Green gate per core-side story (US-001..US-004): `cargo build -p rivetkit-core` plus any inline `cargo test -p rivetkit-core` tests added in that story.
- Green gate per NAPI Rust story (US-005..US-013): `cargo build -p rivetkit-napi` (the adapter crate at `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit-napi`) plus inline `cargo test` if applicable.
- Green gate per TS story (US-014..US-016): `pnpm build -F rivetkit` from the TS workspace root, then `pnpm --filter @rivetkit/rivetkit-napi build:force` when the `.node` needs to refresh, then targeted driver tests via `pnpm test` from `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`.
- After NAPI Rust changes, ALWAYS run `pnpm --filter @rivetkit/rivetkit-napi build:force` before any driver test; the normal N-API build skips when a prebuilt `.node` exists.
- Do NOT run workspace-wide `cargo build` or `cargo test`. Unrelated crates may be red; that's fine.
- Do NOT write backward-compat shims. This is a hard cutover at the NAPI boundary.

===== DESIGN INVARIANTS =====

- Core owns zero user-level tasks. Adapter owns user tasks via a `JoinSet`.
- Per-conn event causality is a core guarantee; adapter spawns per event without re-implementing a per-conn queue.
- `run` handler is NON-FATAL: log Ok/Err, do not cancel actor, do not save state. `ctx.restartRunHandler()` aborts current handle and respawns.
- `AbortSignal` is synthesized at NAPI on top of a `CancellationToken`. Cancelled ONLY on `Destroy` and adapter end-of-life. NOT cancelled on `Sleep` or `run` exit.
- Sleep sequence: drain â†’ `onSleep` â†’ drain â†’ inline `onDisconnect` per non-hibernatable â†’ `ctx.disconnect_conns` â†’ (if dirty) `ctx.save_state(deltas)` â†’ reply.
- Destroy sequence: `abort.cancel()` â†’ `onDestroy` â†’ drain â†’ inline `onDisconnect` per conn â†’ `ctx.disconnect_conns(|_| true)` â†’ (if dirty) `ctx.save_state(deltas)` â†’ reply.
- `onDisconnect` during shutdown runs inline (NOT via mailbox). `ctx.disconnect_conn(s)` is transport-teardown only and fires no `ConnectionClosed` events.
- Three-phase connect: `onBeforeConnect` (no conn) â†’ `createConnState` â†’ `onConnect`, all chained inside one `ConnectionOpen` arm.
- Dirty flag is flipped JS-side by `@rivetkit/on-change` proxy handler; handler calls `ctx.requestSave(false)`. Flags are cleared inside `serializeForTick(reason)` for `save|sleep|destroy` but NOT for `inspector`.
- `SerializeState` is a single event with a `reason` (Save | Inspector). Sleep/Destroy termination events carry `Reply<()>` only â€” adapter persists explicitly via `ctx.save_state(deltas)` if anything is dirty.
- `Action.conn` is `Option<ConnHandle>` â€” `None` for alarm-originated actions. User actions must tolerate no-conn dispatch.
- Per-callback timeouts wrap every TSF with `tokio::time::timeout` using the matching `JsActorConfig.*TimeoutMs` value.
- Every `Reply<T>` is drop-guarded. Spawned-task panics or abort cancellations send `Err(actor_shutting_down())` via the `select!`.

===== GOAL =====

After US-016 lands, the NAPI adapter runs a Rust-side receive loop that:
  1. Consumes `ActorEvent`s from `ActorStart.events`.
  2. Dispatches each to the correct TSF against the pre-built `CallbackBindings`.
  3. Handles three-phase connect, action wrapping, dirty-flag serialization, inspector overlay, sleep/destroy ordering, and non-fatal `run` exactly as specified.
  4. Exposes `ctx.saveState({immediate, maxWait})`, `ctx.abortSignal()`, `ctx.restartRunHandler()`, `ctx.keepAwake(promise)`, and `ctx.isReady()` / `ctx.isStarted()` on the JS ctx wrapper, backed by the adapter token + JoinSet.
  5. Passes the existing driver test suite (`rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`) when run with `pnpm test`.

The TS public actor-authoring API is unchanged from `feat/sqlite-vfs-v2`. User actors that work at that ref continue to work unmodified.
